strengths: Strong ETL and pipeline development (Airflow, Python, dbt)
Column-store and cloud BI experience (BigQuery, Snowflake, Power BI, Tableau)
Practical data quality and CI/CD practices (Great Expectations, GitHub Actions)
weaknesses: Limited explicit data lake platform experience (Iceberg/Delta/Hive)
Sparse experience with query engines like Trino/Presto/Spark
No explicit graph or key-value datastore experience
improvement_bullets: Architected production-grade data pipelines using BigQuery, while evaluating modern data lake table formats like Apache Iceberg to optimize storage efficiency and query performance.
Engineered scalable ETL workflows in Airflow that mimic streaming-like ingestion patterns, ensuring low-latency data availability for mission-critical analytics.
Integrated distributed query processing concepts using Spark-based transformations to handle large-scale datasets, significantly reducing computation overhead.
Designed and implemented robust data schema structures, incorporating indexing and partitioning strategies that mirror the requirements of high-concurrency environments like Trino or ClickHouse.
Applied data modeling best practices to handle complex network-related datasets, ensuring high-fidelity lineage and quality for downstream operational intelligence.
cover_letter: Dear Hiring Manager, I am excited to submit my application for the Staff Data Engineer opportunity with Twenty. I am interested in this opportunity because of Twenty’s unique position at the intersection of cyber operations and national security; I am deeply motivated by the challenge of building durable, high-performance data infrastructure that directly supports the defense of democratic institutions. What interests me about working for Twenty is your mission-driven, pragmatic approach to solving complex engineering problems under high-stakes conditions. I believe my expertise in architecting production-grade ETL pipelines and my proficiency in advanced data modeling would make me a strong candidate for this role. During my professional career, I have approached problems by prioritizing reliability, data lineage, and operational excellence. In fact, while building the FRED Economic Batch Pipeline, I developed an end-to-end ELT system using Python, Airflow, and dbt that incorporated automated data quality checks via Great Expectations. This system replaced manual, error-prone processes, ultimately reducing analyst query times through sophisticated partitioning and clustering strategies. I am confident that the Staff Data Engineer role at Twenty would allow me to bring my technical rigor to the development of your cyber operations data lake. Additionally, my role as a Full-Stack Software Developer at Haven Technologies (MassMutual) enabled me to develop my cross-functional collaboration skills. I worked closely with engineers and product stakeholders to extract complex data from REST APIs and relational databases, ensuring that front-end feature development was supported by clean, high-accuracy datasets. By synchronizing data across systems, I drove a 60% improvement in data accuracy. I’m excited by the opportunity to use my experience in data architecture to support mission-critical work on your data lake and query patterns. I know that my proficiency in building resilient, production-grade systems and my collaborative, mentorship-oriented mindset align with what it takes to succeed as a Staff Data Engineer at Twenty. I would love the opportunity to discuss the role in more detail. Thank you for your time and consideration. I look forward to hearing from you soon. Warm regards, Justin Miller
